assertions.expectation: Proposes a phased approach: control plane first, then CPU pools, then T4 dev, then inference (A100), then training (H100) last
assertions.reason: Response clearly outlines phased approach with control plane first (Phase 1), CPU services and T4 dev (Phase 1), A100 inference (Phase 2), and H100 training last (Phase 3)

assertions.expectation: Control plane can be upgraded independently without affecting running workloads
assertions.reason: Explicitly mentions control plane upgrade with 48-hour soak period before node upgrades, indicating understanding that control plane upgrades don't affect running workloads

assertions.expectation: CPU service pools upgraded first as lowest risk — use standard surge settings (maxSurge=2-3)
assertions.reason: CPU services are upgraded first with maxSurge=5% (50 nodes) and maxUnavailable=0, which is within reasonable surge parameters for low-risk workloads

assertions.expectation: T4 dev pool upgraded next — dev workloads are tolerant of interruption
assertions.reason: T4 development nodes are upgraded after CPU services with higher parallelism (maxSurge=10%, maxUnavailable=2), acknowledging dev workloads can tolerate interruption

assertions.expectation: A100 inference pool upgraded with careful surge settings (maxSurge=1) and PDBs to maintain serving capacity
assertions.reason: While A100 inference is treated carefully with conservative surge (15 nodes) or blue-green strategy, there is no mention of PodDisruptionBudgets (PDBs) to maintain serving capacity

assertions.expectation: H100 training pool upgraded last, during a scheduled gap between training runs — with cordon-and-wait or maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=1
assertions.reason: H100 training is upgraded last with explicit coordination for training gaps, uses blue-green strategy which effectively implements cordon-and-wait approach, though doesn't use exact maxSurge=0/maxUnavailable=1 settings

assertions.expectation: Notes the 20-node parallelism limit — at 5,000 nodes, the full upgrade will take weeks and must span multiple maintenance windows
assertions.reason: Response mentions 20-node parallelism for CPU services and provides a 5-week timeline with multiple maintenance windows spanning the entire upgrade

assertions.expectation: Mentions GPU driver compatibility testing in staging before each GPU pool upgrade
assertions.reason: While the response includes multi-cluster coordination mentioning dev/staging clusters, it does not explicitly mention GPU driver compatibility testing in staging before GPU pool upgrades

assertions.expectation: Addresses GPUDirect/RDMA networking validation after upgrading the H100 pool
assertions.reason: Explicitly includes H100-specific validations section with commands to verify GPUDirect-TCPX functionality and check RDMA interface status
